title: MCP Server
description: The Podo UI MCP server allows AI coding tools to directly query components, style systems, and usage examples.

setup.title: Setup
setup.description: Register the Podo UI MCP server with your AI coding tool. Replace the path with your actual podo-ui installation path.

note.title: Build Required
note.content: Before using the MCP server, run npm run build in the mcp folder to generate dist/index.js.

toolList.title: Available Tools
toolList.description: The MCP server provides 6 tools for AI tools to query podo-ui information.

tools.getOverview: Get Podo UI overview including version, component list, installation methods, themes, and tech stack.
tools.searchComponent: Search components by name or keyword. Returns matching components with name, category, and description.
tools.getComponent: Get detailed component info including props, CSS classes, usage examples, and related components.
tools.getSystem: Get SCSS system details. Supports color tokens, spacing scale, typography, grid, icons, and button styles.
tools.searchCssClass: Search by CSS class name to find which component or system defines it.
tools.getExample: Search usage examples by keyword. Can be filtered by framework: React, Svelte, SCSS, or HTML.

example.title: Usage Examples
example.description: With the MCP server connected, you can ask AI tools about Podo UI in natural language.
example.prompt1: Show me the props and usage of the Podo UI Input component
example.prompt2: Show me a form validation example using Podo UI
example.prompt3: List the primary color tokens in the Podo UI color system
